hook.text: 'Chainsaw Al' Dunlap was hired to save Sunbeam. Instead, he cooked the books.

buildup.text: Sunbeam shipped millions in products to retailers who never ordered them—booking instant revenue.

discovery.text: This is 'channel stuffing': forcing inventory onto distributors to inflate sales numbers.

twist.text: When returns flooded back, the revenue evaporated and the fraud became impossible to hide.

climax.text: Dunlap was fired, fined $15 million, and permanently banned from serving as a corporate officer.

punchline.text: Fake sales always come back. Literally.

quiz.question: What is channel stuffing?
options: Shipping unordered products to inflate revenue
